milestones.vision: Enable users to organize their belongings into collections and add objects to them. This establishes the core data model and UI patterns that all other features will build upon.
success_criteria: User can view a list of their collections with cover images and object counts
User can create a new collection with name and description
User can view collection details and list objects within it
User can add an object to a collection with name, condition, and optional metadata
Objects display with proper VHS 80s styling
All CRUD operations persist to database via tRPC

key_risks.risk: QR stock generation may conflict if UUIDs aren't deterministic
key_risks.whyItMatters: QR codes must be unique and printable

key_risks.risk: Image upload for covers requires S3 setup
key_risks.whyItMatters: UI incomplete without visual collection covers

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Objects and collections persist after page refresh
User can navigate from dashboard → collection → object without errors

milestones.verification_contract: E2E tests pass for: create collection, add object to collection, view collection list
milestones.verification_integration: tRPC routers handle all CRUD operations without errors. Prisma queries use proper includes to avoid N+1.
milestones.verification_operational: Static pages render without hydration errors. Console has no Error-level logs.
milestones.verification_uat: User can create a collection named 'Mes Livres' and add an object 'Le Petit Prince' to it.
definition_of_done: Add object form (/objects/add with collection selection)
milestones.requirement_coverage: R001: Collections management - full CRUD\nR002: Objects management - create and list within collections

slices.title: Collections CRUD
slices.demo: After this: User can create and browse collections from dashboard
slices.full_summary_md:

# S01: Collections CRUD — UAT

**Milestone:** M001
**Written:** 2026-04-07T19:45:39.076Z

### View Collections
Navigate to /collections - see list of collections with name, description, object count
